name: pd_incidents
description: List all currently active PagerDuty incidents (status: triggered or acknowledged), sorted by urgency then age. Use this to answer 'what's firing?' or 'what's on fire?' questions. This tool shows current state only; for historical patterns, use pd_recent instead. For detailed per-incident information including timeline and alerts, use pd_incident_detail.

name: pd_incident_detail
description: Get full details for a specific incident including timeline (log entries), related alerts, notes, and basic analysis. Requires a valid incident_id obtained from pd_incidents, pd_recent, or pd_incident_note output. Use this after identifying a specific incident to investigate. Do not use this for bulk listing; use pd_incidents for that.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID in format P123ABC (e.g., P123ABC, PXYZ789). Obtain from pd_incidents or pd_recent output.

name: pd_oncall
description: Show who is currently on-call for all schedules and escalation policies. Use this to answer 'who's on call?' or 'who's oncall right now?' questions. Returns user name, email, schedule, escalation policy, and shift start/end times for each on-call assignment. This reflects current state only; it does not show future or past on-call assignments.

name: pd_services
description: List all PagerDuty services with their current operational status. Status values are: critical (has active triggered incident), warning (has active acknowledged incident), active (operational, no active incidents), or disabled. Use this to get an overview of service health or to find service IDs for use with pd_recent. Do not use this to check current incidents; use pd_incidents instead.

name: pd_recent
description: Show historical incidents for a service or across all services within a time window, with summary statistics. Use this to answer 'what happened last night?' or to investigate incident patterns over time. For currently active incidents, use pd_incidents instead. The optional service parameter filters by service ID (obtain from pd_services); without it, returns incidents for all services.

service.description: Optional. Filter to a specific PagerDuty service ID (e.g., PSVC123). Obtain from pd_services output. If omitted, returns incidents for all services.

since.description: Time window for historical lookup. Valid values: 24h (last 24 hours), 7d (last 7 days), 30d (last 30 days). Default: 24h.

name: pd_incident_ack
description: Acknowledge a triggered incident, stopping further escalation. Use this when you have confirmed the incident and are actively working on it. Requires the --confirm flag to be true AND the PAGERDUTY_EMAIL environment variable to be set. Without --confirm, this tool returns a confirmation preview instead of executing. For resolving incidents (marking as fixed), use pd_incident_resolve instead. For viewing incidents without modifying them, use pd_incidents.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to acknowledge (e.g., P123ABC). Must be a currently triggered incident; acknowledged or resolved incidents cannot be re-acknowledged.

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually acknowledge the incident. When false or omitted, returns a preview of what would happen without executing the write operation.

name: pd_incident_resolve
description: Resolve an incident, marking it as fixed. Use this when the underlying issue has been resolved and the incident can be closed. Requires the --confirm flag to be true AND the PAGERDUTY_EMAIL environment variable to be set. Without --confirm, this tool returns a confirmation preview instead of executing. For acknowledging incidents (stopping escalation while investigating), use pd_incident_ack instead. For viewing incidents without modifying them, use pd_incidents.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to resolve (e.g., P123ABC). Can resolve triggered or acknowledged incidents. Resolved incidents are closed and excluded from pd_incidents output.

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually resolve the incident. When false or omitted, returns a preview of what would happen without executing the write operation.

name: pd_incident_note
description: Add a permanent note to an incident's timeline. Use this to document root cause analysis, investigation steps, remediation actions, or any context useful for future reference. Notes become part of the incident's permanent record and are visible in pd_incident_detail. Requires the --confirm flag to be true AND the PAGERDUTY_EMAIL environment variable to be set. Without --confirm, this tool returns a confirmation preview instead of executing. For viewing incidents, use pd_incidents or pd_incident_detail instead.

incident_id.description: The PagerDuty incident ID to add a note to (e.g., P123ABC). The incident can be in any status (triggered, acknowledged, or resolved).

content.description: The note text to add to the incident timeline. This is a free-form string field; include any relevant details such as root cause, investigation steps, remediation actions, or links to runbooks.

confirm.description: Must be set to true to actually add the note. When false or omitted, returns a preview of what would happen without executing the write operation.
